CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-26-2016 Continued To Dec-14-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-15-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF JANE ROE'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A Second Amended Complaint is DENIED because defendants' demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's arguments are essentially the same as on the demurrer. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-15-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS YARDI SYSTEMS, INC. and RENTGROW, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. The complaint asserts violations of the Investigative Consumer Reporting Agencies Act. Defendants point to no less than four court decisions finding the ICRAA unconstitutionally vague because it conflicts with the Consumer Credit Reporting Agencies Act: Ortiz v. Lyon Mgmt. Group, Inc. (2007) 157 Cal.App.4th 604; Trujillo v. First American Registry (2007) 157 Cal.App.4th 628; Moran v. Screening Pros, LLC, 2012 WL 10655744 (C.D. Cal.); Jane Roe v. LexisNexis Risk Solutions, Inc., 2013 WL 112469904 (C.D. Cal.). Plaintiff says these decisions are "flawed" and "incorrect." However, Ortiz and Trujillo have been law for almost a decade now, Moran is in precisely our case's context, and plaintiff points to no opinion contrary to any of them. The Court does not have discretion to ignore settled law. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)
